label: 93_patent_patents_patent rights_patenting
description: The patent system faces criticism for potentially stifling innovation through ineffective screening. Economists argue that underinvestment in research and development is common, with social returns on innovation often exceeding private ones. Successful inventions are seen as valuable but costly to develop, highlighting the need for proper protection mechanisms.

newest_articles.title: Imitation and the diffusion of innovation
newest_articles.excerpt: Imitation and the diffusion of innovation Summary Focus When a firm develops a new technology, conventional wisdom suggests it should protect that innovation through patents or licensing. central_articles.title: Patent screening, innovation, and welfare
central_articles.excerpt: In the last 15 years, there has been substantial pushback against the patent system. Critics claim that patent rights are becoming an impediment to innovation rather than an incentive, and an instrument to extract rents through patent litigation. One of the main culprits identified by critics is ineffective screening…
